properties.user_story: As an Analyst, I want to open an .excalidraw board from the sidebar so that I can inspect or edit its content in a full-featured canvas.
properties.name: Open Board in Editor
acceptance_criteria: Given a board exists on disk, when I click it in the sidebar, then GET /boards/:name returns 200 with the file contents.
Given the board loads, when the Excalidraw canvas mounts, then the elements are visible.
Given an unknown board name, when I request it, then the server returns 404.
Given a board is selected, when the URL changes, then the active board is reflected in the StatusBar.

properties.user_story: As an Analyst, I want my canvas edits to be auto-saved so that I never lose work and the board state on disk reflects my latest changes.
properties.name: Edit and Save Board
acceptance_criteria: Given the canvas is loaded, when I edit elements, then a debounced PUT /boards/:name fires with the new payload.
Given a save is in flight, when it succeeds, then the board status transitions from dirty to clean.
Given a save fails, when the error is returned, then the StatusBar surfaces the error and retains dirty state.
Given concurrent edits, when the server receives them, then the last-write-wins payload is persisted.

properties.user_story: As an Analyst, I want to regenerate a board from the Neo4j graph so that the diagram reflects the current source-of-truth model.
properties.name: Regenerate Board from Graph
acceptance_criteria: Given a renderable board, when I click Regenerate, then the server invokes the deterministic renderer via the skill runner.
Given an import-only board, when it is selected, then the Regenerate button is disabled.
Given Regenerate succeeds, when the run completes, then the StatusBar shows an updated last-generated timestamp.
Given Regenerate is already running, when I click again, then the button is disabled until the prior run finishes.

properties.user_story: As an Analyst, I want to push my board edits back to the Neo4j graph so that downstream consumers see the new state.
properties.name: Sync Board to Graph
acceptance_criteria: Given a dirty board, when I click Sync, then skill-runner invokes /nacl-ba-sync via the itsalt-pinch Pacer.
Given Sync completes, when the run record is written, then the StatusBar shows an updated last-synced timestamp.
Given the Pacer wrapper is unavailable, when Sync is triggered, then the server returns a structured error.
Given Sync is in progress, when I navigate away, then the run continues and is reflected in run history.

properties.user_story: As an Analyst, I want to switch the active NaCl project from the project picker so that the boards, graph, and skills resolve to the project I care about.
properties.name: Switch Active Project
acceptance_criteria: Given multiple registered projects, when I open the picker, then GET /projects lists them.
Given I select a project, when activate is called, then ~/.nacl/projects.json marks it active and the sidebar reloads.
Given the cwd has config.yaml but is not registered, when the app loads, then an Unregistered banner is shown.
Given the registry is empty and no cwd config, when the app loads, then the empty-projects placeholder is shown.

properties.user_story: As an Analyst, I want to browse historical snapshots of a board, view a diff against the current version, and restore an earlier state.
properties.name: View and Restore Snapshot
acceptance_criteria: Given a board with no snapshots, when I open the browser, then an empty state is shown.
Given a snapshot file exists on disk, when the browser refreshes, then the snapshot is listed with its timestamp.
Given I select a snapshot, when I view diff, then the differences against the current board are visualised.
Given I click Restore, when the confirmation is accepted, then the snapshot content overwrites the current board.

properties.user_story: As an Analyst, I want to render a markdown document from the graph so that specs (BA/SA) stay in lockstep with the graph model.
properties.name: Render Markdown from Graph
acceptance_criteria: Given a renderable markdown target, when I trigger render, then the server walks the deterministic pipeline in server/src/render/markdown.
Given render succeeds, when the response returns, then the markdown file on disk is updated atomically.
Given the graph is empty for the requested document, when render runs, then an explicit no-content outcome is returned.
Given a render error, when surfaced, then the run record captures the failure with stderr.

properties.user_story: As an Analyst, I want to see all boards in the active project grouped by kind so that I can quickly find the one to work on.
properties.name: List Boards
acceptance_criteria: Given the active project resolves, when GET /boards is called, then the response lists boards grouped (e.g. Imports).
Given a board is added on disk, when the sidebar refreshes, then the new board appears.
Given no boards exist, when listed, then an empty array is returned.

properties.user_story: As an Analyst, I want the tool to know which boards are renderable from the graph so that Regenerate is offered only where it is safe.
properties.name: Discover Renderable Boards
acceptance_criteria: Given a board mapped to a deterministic renderer, when discovery runs, then it is flagged renderable=true.
Given an Imports board with no renderer, when discovery runs, then it is flagged renderable=false.
Given discovery results, when consumed by the UI, then the Regenerate button enablement matches the flag.

properties.user_story: As an Analyst, I want to see last-generated and last-synced timestamps for a board so that I know how fresh it is.
properties.name: Get Board Status
acceptance_criteria: Given a board with prior runs, when its status is fetched, then last_generated and last_synced are returned.
Given a board with no runs, when status is fetched, then both timestamps are null.
Given a successful regenerate, when status is re-fetched, then last_generated is updated.
